title: W8 B Multidim Concave Functions
body:
Mathematical Economics

Juan Carlos Carbajal

School of Economics UNSWвҖ“Sydney

Week 8B вҖ” Term 1, 2026

1/9  Concave and Convex Functions in Rn

2/9   Concave and Convex Functions

Definition 1

Let S вҠҶ Rn be a convex set and f : S вҶ’ R a real-valued function. (a) f is said to be a concave function if for all x, y вҲҲ S and all Оұ вҲҲ (0, 1) one has  f Оұx + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)y вүҘ Оұf (x) + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)f (y).

(b) f is said to be a convex function if for all x, y вҲҲ S and all Оұ вҲҲ (0, 1) one has  f Оұx + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)y вүӨ Оұf (x) + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)f (y).

Remark: the notion of concave or convex functions only makes sense under a convex domain.

3/9   Examples

Example 1

(a) The Euclidean distance to z, dz : Rn вҶ’ R+ defined by dz (x) = вҲҘx вҲ’ zвҲҘ, is a convex function.

(b) The Cobb-Douglas function u(x1 , x2 ) = xОұ 1 Оұ2 2 1 x2 defined on R++ is concave, provided Оұ1 , Оұ2 вүҘ 0 and Оұ1 + Оұ2 вүӨ 1.

(c) The Leontieff function v(x1 , x2 ) = min{x1 , x2 } defined on R2+ is concave.

(d) The linear function w(x1 , x2 ) = c1 x2 + c2 x2 defined on R2 is both concave and convex.

4/9   Checking Concavity and Convexity

Sometimes it is simple to verify concavity or convexity of a function directly via definitions.

Lemma 1

The Euclidean norm вҲҘ В· вҲҘ in Rn is a convex function.

Proof It follows directly from the triangle inequality. For any Оұ вҲҲ (0, 1),

вҲҘОұ x + (1 вҲ’ Оұ) yвҲҘ вүӨ ОұвҲҘxвҲҘ + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)вҲҘyвҲҘ.

Sometimes it is easier to take an alternative route.

5/9   Playing with Concavity and Convexity

Proposition 1

Let S вҠҶ Rn be a convex set and f : S вҶ’ R, g : S вҶ’ R be two concave functions. Then (a) The function f + g is concave. (b) If c вүҘ 0, then c f is concave. (c) If c вүӨ 0, then c f is convex.

Proof (a) Let x, y вҲҲ S and Оұ вҲҲ (0, 1). Then    (f + g) Оұx + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)y = f Оұx + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)y + g Оұx + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)y

вүҘ Оұf (x) + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)f (y) + Оұg(x) + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)g(y)

= Оұ(f + g)(x) + (1 вҲ’ Оұ)(f + g)(y).

Parts (b) and (c) are left as exercises.

6/9   Playing with Concavity and Convexity

Proposition 2

Let f : S вҶ’ R and g : I вҶ’ R, where S вҠҶ Rn and I вҠҶ R are convex sets, and the range f (S) вҠҶ I. If f and g are concave, and g is weakly increasing, then the composite function h = g в—Ұ f is concave on S.

Proof Let x, y вҲҲ S and О» вҲҲ (0, 1). Then   h О»x + (1 вҲ’ О»)y = g f О»x + (1 вҲ’ О»)y  вүҘ g О»f (x) + (1 вҲ’ О»)f (y)

7/9   Playing with Concavity and Convexity

Proposition 2

Let f : S вҶ’ R and g : I вҶ’ R, where S вҠҶ Rn and I вҠҶ R are convex sets, and the range f (S) вҠҶ I. If f and g are concave, and g is weakly increasing, then the composite function h = g в—Ұ f is concave on S.

Proof Let x, y вҲҲ S and О» вҲҲ (0, 1). Then   h О»x + (1 вҲ’ О»)y = g f О»x + (1 вҲ’ О»)y  вүҘ g О»f (x) + (1 вҲ’ О»)f (y)   вүҘ О» g f (x) + (1 вҲ’ О») g f (y)

= О» h(x) + (1 вҲ’ О») h(y).

7/9   Checking Concavity and Convexity

Let f (x1 , x2 ) = xОұ Оұ2 2 1 x2 be defined on R++ , with Оұj вүҘ 0 and Оұ1 + Оұ2 вүӨ 1.

The Hessian matrix of f is пЈ® 2 2 пЈ№ пЈ® Оұ (Оұ вҲ’1) пЈ№ вҲӮ f (x) вҲӮ f (x) 1 1 Оұ1 Оұ2 2 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 x2 x1 x2 D f (x) = пЈ° пЈ» = f (x) пЈ° пЈ». вҲӮ 2 f (x) вҲӮ 2 f (x) Оұ1 Оұ2 Оұ2 (Оұ2 вҲ’1) вҲӮx2 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 вҲӮx2 x1 x2 x2

8/9   Checking Concavity and Convexity

Let f (x1 , x2 ) = xОұ Оұ2 2 1 x2 be defined on R++ , with Оұj вүҘ 0 and Оұ1 + Оұ2 вүӨ 1.

The Hessian matrix of f is пЈ® 2 2 пЈ№ пЈ® Оұ (Оұ вҲ’1) пЈ№ вҲӮ f (x) вҲӮ f (x) 1 1 Оұ1 Оұ2 2 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 x2 x1 x2 D f (x) = пЈ° пЈ» = f (x) пЈ° пЈ». вҲӮ 2 f (x) вҲӮ 2 f (x) Оұ1 Оұ2 Оұ2 (Оұ2 вҲ’1) вҲӮx2 вҲӮx1 вҲӮx2 вҲӮx2 x1 x2 x2

The вҖҳsignвҖҷ of the Hessian matrix is determined by whether this is positive semidefinite or negative semidefinite.

In this case, note Оұ1 (Оұx12 вҲ’1) вүӨ 0, and

Оұ1 (Оұ1 вҲ’ 1) Оұ2 (Оұ2 вҲ’ 1) Оұ1 Оұ2 Оұ1 Оұ2 Оұ1 Оұ2 вҲ’ = 2 2 (1 вҲ’ Оұ1 вҲ’ Оұ2 ) вүҘ 0. x21 x22 x1 x2 x1 x2 x1 x2

D2 f (x) is negative semi-definite.

8/9   Concavity/Convexity and Differentiability

Proposition 3

Let S вҠҶ Rn be open and convex and f : S вҶ’ R be twice continuously differentiable. Then (a) f is concave on S if and only if D2 f (x) is negative semi-definite, for all x вҲҲ S; (b) f is convex on S if and only if D2 f (x) is positive semi-definite, for all x вҲҲ S; (c) if D2 f (x) is negative definite for all x вҲҲ S, then f is strictly concave on S; (d) if D2 f (x) is positive definite for all x вҲҲ S, then f is strictly convex on S.

Proof Omitted

9/9
